name: Stoichiometric mass and atom balance in chemical reactions
conventions: stoichiometric coefficients nu are positive integers (or rational with a common denominator)
each side balanced per element: sum nu * count(element) reactants = sum nu * count(element) products
each side balanced for total charge: sum nu * charge reactants = sum nu * charge products
phase labels (s, l, g, aq) do not affect balance — only atom and charge counts do
expectedLimits: closed system: total mass conserved exactly (delta m = 0)
each element: atom count conserved exactly
ionic / redox reactions: total charge conserved exactly
nuclear reactions: mass-energy conservation replaces atom conservation (E = delta m c^2)
references: Dalton (1808) — A New System of Chemical Philosophy
IUPAC — Quantities, Units and Symbols in Physical Chemistry
